build_attempt_summary: Multiple rebuild attempts made. arm64 iOS Simulator build blocked by CocosCreator prebuilt native library incompatibility with Xcode 15+/SDK 26.4. x86_64 Simulator build succeeded but was rejected by iPhone 17 simulator (requires arm64 only).
code_gaps: CocosCreator prebuilt native libraries (libssl.a, libcrypto.a) in both ios/libs/ and ios-m1-simulator/libs/ use old LC_VERSION_MIN_IPHONEOS format for arm64 slice (platform 2 = iOS device), but Xcode 15+ SDK 26.4 linker requires LC_BUILD_VERSION with platform 7 (iOS Simulator) for arm64. This causes linker error: 'building for iOS Simulator, but linking in object file built for iOS'. This is a CocosCreator engine issue requiring updated prebuilt libraries.

x86_64 iOS Simulator build compiled successfully but installation on iPhone 17 simulator (D9F5F3CA) failed with error 'Needs to Be Updated' - the simulator requires arm64, not x86_64 on Apple Silicon Macs.
Workaround path: regenerate iOS Xcode project via Cocos Creator dashboard after CocosCreator is updated, OR wait for CocosCreator to provide arm64-simulator-compatible ssl/crypto prebuilt libs.
Alternative acceptance path: web-mobile build exists at game-client/build/web-mobile/ and could be used for browser-based smoke testing while iOS Simulator issue is resolved.

environment_blockers.blocker: CocosCreator native prebuilt lib arm64 platform mismatch
environment_blockers.detail: ios-m1-simulator/libs/libssl.a and libcrypto.a are arm64 but tagged as iOS device (LC_VERSION_MIN_IPHONEOS) not iOS Simulator (LC_BUILD_VERSION platform=7). Xcode 15/SDK 26.4 linker rejects this for iphonesimulator target.
environment_blockers.linker_error: ld: in .../libssl.a(bio_ssl.o), building for iOS Simulator, but linking in object file built for iOS
